title: Maya's Drama Exam
story_html:
Maya had one week left before her drama class exam. Every student had to perform a short scene in front of a small audience, and Maya was given the part of a clever thief in an old folk story.

Her drama guide, Ms. Lopez, told the class they had to move like real characters, not just say the words. Maya practiced being graceful, sneaking across the room with soft steps, while her friend Ben played the guard — her character's silly enemy in the play.

At first, Maya kept forgetting her lines, or moving the wrong way toward a small pretend box that held the treasure. Ms. Lopez was nice about it, reminding Maya that a mistake was simple to fix if she stayed calm.

Each afternoon, the group rehearsed outside on the grass behind the school, since the classroom felt too small. Maya grew more confident every day, though she still felt shy whenever people watched.

On the big day, a huge crowd of parents and teachers filled the room. Maya's heart raced, and for one whole second she froze, forgetting where to stand. Then she remembered to trust herself and Ms. Lopez's advice. She found new strength inside her and stepped forward.

The play had a total of four short scenes, and Maya moved through each one calmly. She reached the wood box, lifted the treasure, and slipped away just as her character was supposed to disappear behind a curtain.

Before she could finish the last line, the whole audience began to cheer. Maya actually smiled, feeling safe and proud for the first time all week. Ms. Lopez gave her a warm thumbs up from the side of the stage.

second.def: A very short amount of time.

part.def: A piece or section of something bigger.

reach.def: To stretch out your hand to touch or get something.

enemy.def: A person who is against you.

trust.def: To believe that someone is honest and reliable.

graceful.def: Moving in a smooth and beautiful way.

finish.def: To complete something; to reach the end of it.

confident.def: Feeling sure about your own abilities.

guide.def: A person who shows the way.

group.def: A number of people or things together.

still.def: Continuing up to the present time.

wood.def: Trees, or the material from trees.

total.def: Complete; adding up to the whole amount.

grass.def: The green plants that cover the ground.

strength.def: The quality of being physically or mentally strong.

exam.def: A test of what someone knows or can do.

cheer.def: To shout or clap to show support or happiness.

disappear.def: To go away and not be seen.

story.def: A telling of events, real or made up.

keep.def: To have and hold onto something.

questions.qtext: In "Maya practiced being graceful, sneaking across the room with soft steps", what does graceful mean?
options: moving in a smooth, beautiful way
questions.explain: A — graceful means moving in a smooth and beautiful way, which is why Maya practiced soft, careful steps.

questions.qtext: In "her friend Ben played the guard — her character's silly enemy in the play", what does enemy mean?
options: a person who is against you
questions.explain: B — an enemy is a person who is against you, which fits the guard's role opposite the thief character.

questions.qtext: In "She found new strength inside her and stepped forward", what does strength mean?
questions.explain: B — strength means being physically or mentally strong, which helped Maya move past freezing with fear.

questions.qtext: What happened right after Maya froze for one second during her exam?
options: She ran off the stage crying
She remembered to trust herself and kept going
Ms. Lopez took over her part
questions.explain: B — the story says Maya remembered to trust herself and Ms. Lopez's advice, then found strength to step forward.

questions.qtext: How did Maya feel by the end of her performance?
options: Embarrassed that she forgot a line
Angry at Ben for playing the guard
Safe, proud, and happy for the first time all week
questions.explain: C — the story ends with Maya smiling and feeling safe and proud after the audience cheered for her.
